jd_text: About the Company A Projects Director leads high-value construction projects from inception to handover, ensuring compliance with budget, schedule, and quality standards. About the Role Key responsibilities include strategic planning, stakeholder management, risk mitigation, and team leadership, often requiring experience with local Qatar regulations. Responsibilities Strategic Leadership: Directing project execution plans, defining project scope, goals, and deliverables in alignment with senior management. Financial & Contractual Management: Managing project budgets, approving expenditures, negotiating contracts, and handling variation orders. Stakeholder & Client Relations: Serving as the primary point of contact for clients, consultants, and contractors to ensure project alignment. Risk & Quality Management: Implementing risk mitigation strategies, ensuring adherence to health, safety, and environmental (HSE) standards, and overseeing quality control. Team Management: Leading and mentoring project managers, engineers, and technical staff. Qualifications Experience: Minimum 20-25 years of extensive experience in project management, with a proven track record of managing large-scale projects for high-rise buildings 50-60 stories and above the GCC and wider. Education: Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or Architecture; a PMP certification is preferred. Required Skills Strong leadership Commercial acumen Negotiation abilities In-depth knowledge of local Dubai municipality regulations Preferred Skills Experience with local Qatar regulations Pay range and compensation package Details on pay range or salary or compensation will be provided during the interview process. Equal Opportunity Statement We are committed to diversity and inclusivity in our hiring practices.
